unit.note: server: 211 files, 2467 passed + 1 skipped (adds event-log-recovery.test.ts). client: 299 files, 2749 passed (untouched — zero client source changed). tsc --noEmit clean; oxlint reports no warnings for any changed file. Critically, ALL 66 pre-existing tests across event-log-reader/run-data-join/campaign-events pass UNCHANGED, including the ones pinning exact skippedLines counts — the counters keep their line-based meaning.

integration.note: event-log-recovery.test.ts drives all THREE independent projections over the same bytes and asserts they agree on how many records a damaged line holds — the invariant that two separate scans of one file must not disagree.

e2e.note: No API shape or client file changed: the endpoints return the same envelope, only with records that were previously dropped now present. The observable surface is covered at the projection level, where the defect lives. The prior iterate (#291) ran a web-surface E2E because the triage fix changed what the Inbox renders; here no client code is touched.

smoke.note: No server started; pure read-path projection change.

surface_verification.runner: npx vitest run src/core/event-log-recovery.test.ts (+ full server suite)
surface_verification.justification: The changed surface is the projection layer behind GET /api/external/events, /runs and /campaigns. It is driven through the exported projections (projectEventLog, projectGradeTrend, projectCampaignEvents) with the concatenated-line shape a git union merge produces. surface=web was NOT claimed: no client file changed and the response envelope is byte-identical, so a browser run would exercise nothing this does not.

behaviors.behavior: projectEventLog recovers BOTH runs from a concatenated line
counters keep line-based meaning: total/skipped count physical lines, parsed counts records
behaviors.evidence: ::counts physical LINES in total/skipped and RECORDS in parsed PASSED

behaviors.behavior: partial damage keeps the valid record AND still counts the line skipped
behaviors.evidence: ::keeps a valid record and still counts the line as skipped PASSED

behaviors.behavior: recovered records take consecutive indices, so last-wins tiebreak is not inverted
behaviors.evidence: ::preserves wire order, so the later of two same-key runs still wins PASSED

behaviors.behavior: a wholly undecodable line is still skipped, not fatal (no regression)
behaviors.evidence: ::still skips a wholly undecodable line PASSED; 66 pre-existing reader tests unchanged

behaviors.behavior: projectGradeTrend recovers snapshots from a concatenated line
behaviors.evidence: ::recovers both snapshots from a concatenated line PASSED

behaviors.behavior: the two passes over the same bytes AGREE on record count
behaviors.evidence: ::agrees with projectEventLog about how many records a damaged line holds PASSED

behaviors.behavior: projectCampaignEvents recovers a sub-iterate completion (prevents a done step rendering as pending)
behaviors.evidence: ::recovers a sub-iterate completion from a concatenated line PASSED

behaviors.behavior: recordsFromLines reports every non-blank physical line once, blanks never
behaviors.evidence: ::reports every non-blank physical line exactly once PASSED

behaviors.behavior: recordsFromLines is safe without a callback
behaviors.evidence: ::is safe to consume without a callback PASSED
